name: Poinsettia
description: Poinsettia (Euphorbia pulcherrima) is a common holiday plant with bright red, pink, or white bracts. It has a scary reputation, but for cats it is usually a mild irritant rather than a severe poison. The main issue is the milky latex sap inside the leaves and stems. This sap contains irritating compounds, including diterpenoid euphorbol esters, that can bother the mouth, stomach, skin, or eyes. Most cats do not eat much because the plant tastes unpleasant, so serious poisoning is uncommon. If a cat chews poinsettia, possible signs include drooling, mouth discomfort, vomiting, diarrhea, lower appetite, or low energy. Sap on the skin or in the eyes can cause redness or irritation. Keep poinsettias out of reach, wipe away fallen leaves, and contact a veterinarian if your cat eats a large amount, keeps vomiting, or has eye irritation.
safetyNotes: Main hazard: The milky latex sap in leaves and stems can irritate the mouth, stomach, skin, and eyes.
Symptoms: Drooling, mouth discomfort, vomiting, diarrhea, reduced appetite, or low energy; skin or eye contact may cause redness.
Risk level: Poinsettia is usually mildly irritating, not one of the highly dangerous holiday plants for cats.
Action: Keep the plant out of reach and call a vet if vomiting continues, eye irritation appears, or your cat ate a large amount.

symptoms.name: Oral irritation, excessive drooling (hypersalivation), and discomfort of the mouth and esophagus
symptoms.notes: This is caused by the thick, milky-white, latex sap found throughout the plant acting as a direct irritant to the mucous membranes1.... 2. Gastrointestinal Distress

symptoms.name: Vomiting, diarrhea, and nausea
symptoms.notes: Actual poisoning from a Poinsettia is considered rare; a cat generally needs to ingest a large amount of the plant to exhibit clinical signs56. The gastrointestinal symptoms are typically self-limiting and rarely require veterinary treatment6. 3. Anorexia

symptoms.name: Anorexia (loss of appetite)
symptoms.notes: This is usually secondary to the gastrointestinal irritation and nausea caused by the plant's sap2.... 4. Depression

symptoms.name: Depression or lethargy
symptoms.notes: This can present alongside the vomiting and anorexia if a significant amount of the plant is ingested6. 5. Cardiovascular Issues

symptoms.name: Cardiovascular issues
symptoms.notes: While Poinsettia toxicity is generally mild and limited to stomach upset, if a cat consumes a significantly large amount of the plant, it may suffer from more serious symptoms, including potential cardiovascular issues
